title: Banisterodes vitellinum
taxonomy: Banisterodes vitellinum is classified within the plant family Menispermaceae. This family comprises around 150 species of flowering plants. Menispermaceae is a part of the larger order Ranunculales, which is a group of flowering plants. The order Ranunculales is further classified under the subclass Magnoliidae, a subcategory of the class Magnoliopsida. Banisterodes vitellinum is a species of plant native to tropical Africa.

intro: Banisterodes vitellinum, also known as African Maythorn, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its woody vine or shrub habit and showy, yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial properties. The bioactive constituents of Banisterodes vitellinum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Banisterodes vitellinum is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used to make topical creams and ointments for skin conditions.
